message:
refactor(vdbe): pass ConcurrentContext to classify_concurrent_write_tier

`classify_concurrent_write_tier` was looking up the concurrent context
itself (via `self.concurrent_context()`) and returning
`Tier2CommitSurfaceRare` when none was found, then returning a
`Result<ConcurrentWriteTier>` even though the body never produced an
error. The single caller in `write_page` already had the
`ConcurrentContext` in hand from the surrounding scope, so the lookup
was redundant.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs:
- Change the signature to
  `fn classify_concurrent_write_tier(&self, ctx: &ConcurrentContext,
   page_no: PageNumber) -> ConcurrentWriteTier`
  (no longer `Result<_>`, takes the ctx by reference).
- Drop the internal `self.concurrent_context()` call and the early
  return for the no-context case.
- The body's three branches (tier 0 / tier 2 / tier 1) now return
  bare values instead of `Ok(...)`.
- The single call site in `write_page` already has `ctx: ConcurrentContext`
  in scope, so it just passes `&ctx` through and drops the `?`.

Behavior is unchanged for any caller that already holds the context,
which was the only caller.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
